title: The Highly Sensitive Person: How to Thrive When the World Overwhelms You
author: Elaine N. Aron
description: Groundbreaking psychology book that introduced the HSP (Highly Sensitive Person) trait — sensory processing sensitivity — to a general audience. Elaine N. Aron's research-based guide helps HSPs understand their innate temperament, the DOES framework, evolutionary advantages, and practical strategies for thriving in an overwhelming world.

keyFigures.name: Elaine N. Aron
keyFigures.note: Pioneer of HSP research; licensed psychotherapist; published 100+ peer-reviewed papers on SPS

keyFigures.name: Arthur Aron
keyFigures.note: Collaborated on early HSP research and the famous '36 Questions' intimacy study

keyFigures.name: Bruce J. Ellis
keyFigures.role: Evolutionary Psychologist, Co-developer of ORCHID/DANDELION Framework
keyFigures.note: Co-authored 2005 paper on differential susceptibility with Thomas Boyce

keyFigures.name: Thomas Boyce
keyFigures.note: Research focused on biological sensitivity to context in child development

bibliography.title: The Highly Sensitive Person: How to Thrive When the World Overwhelms You
bibliography.note: Primary source for this archive entry

bibliography.title: Sensory-processing sensitivity and its relation to introversion and emotionality
bibliography.journal: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
bibliography.note: Foundational peer-reviewed paper establishing HSP as a measurable trait distinguishing it from introversion and neuroticism

bibliography.title: The Highly Sensitive Person: How to Thrive When the World Overwhelms You (updated edition with foreword by the author)
bibliography.note: 2013 reprint with expanded guidance on personal development

bibliography.authors: Boyce, W. T., & Ellis, B. J.
bibliography.title: Biological sensitivity to context: I. An evolutionary-developmental theory of the origins and functions of stress reactivity
bibliography.note: Precursor to ORCHID/DANDELION framework — biological sensitivity to context theory

bibliography.authors: Boyce, W. T., Sokolowski, M. B., & Boyce, W. T.
bibliography.title: Towards a new biology of adversity
bibliography.note: Later popularisation of differential susceptibility; cited by Aron in 2013 foreword

bibliography.authors: Acevedo, B. P., Aron, E. N., Aron, A., Sangras-Hsu, R., & Brown, L.
bibliography.title: The highly sensitive brain: an fMRI study of sensory processing sensitivity and response to others' emotions
bibliography.note: Key fMRI study demonstrating greater ACC and insula activation in HSPs during emotional tasks

bibliography.authors: Greven, C. U., Aron, E. N., & Aron, A.
bibliography.title: Sensory processing sensitivity and the life course
bibliography.note: Comprehensive review of SPS across lifespan and heritability evidence

bibliography.title: Differential susceptibility to long-term effects of quality of child care on externalizing behavior in adolescence
bibliography.note: Cohort study supporting differential susceptibility across development

bibliography.title: Quiet: The Power of Introverts in a World That Can't Stop Talking
bibliography.note: Extension of Aron's HSP framework to introversion more broadly; Aron acknowledged influence in forewords

bibliography.title: The Highly Sensitive Person Self-Test
bibliography.note: Authoritative online version of the HSP Self-Test used in research since 1996
